findings.title: Hardcoded secret key in JWT verification
findings.description: A hardcoded secret 'hardcoded-secret-do-not-use' is used for JWT signing and verification. Secrets should never be hardcoded in source code and must be loaded from environment variables or a secrets manager.
findings.suggestedFix: const SECRET = process.env.JWT_SECRET ?? (() => { throw new Error('JWT_SECRET not set') })();

findings.title: Using 'any' type cast for request user
findings.description: Casting req to 'any' to attach the user bypasses TypeScript's type system. Consider extending the Request type.
findings.suggestedFix:
// Extend express Request type:
declare global { namespace Express { interface Request { user?: JWTPayload } } }

findings.title: SQL injection vulnerability in DELETE endpoint
findings.description: The user ID from the URL parameter is interpolated directly into the SQL query. This allows SQL injection attacks.
findings.suggestedFix: await db.query('DELETE FROM users WHERE id = $1', [id]);

findings.title: Missing authorization check allows any user to delete any account
findings.description: The DELETE /users/:id endpoint only validates authentication but not authorization. Any authenticated user can delete any account including other users' accounts.
findings.suggestedFix: // Check that user can only delete their own account or is an admin

findings.title: Unbounded query on users table
findings.description: SELECT * FROM users without a LIMIT clause will return all records. Add pagination to prevent memory exhaustion.
